title: unshare: some cleanups and define CONTAINERS_{RUNROOT,GRAPHROOT}
body:
define two environment variables, that simplify the task of cleaning up the storage, as we can do something like:

podman unshare sh -c 'rm -rf $CONTAINERS_GRAPHROOT $CONTAINERS_RUNROOT'

comments.body:

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I am fine with this, but I would prefer a much more direct

podman system storage reset 

Or something like that, which would prompt for user to say he wants it and then destroy storage and configuration back to default.

> podman system storage reset

I agree, we still need something like that.  This is more to have an interactive way to access the storage if for any reason it is required.
